title: spermacoce vulpina
intro: Spermacoce vulpina, also known as Woodruff or False Wormwood, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and distinctive square stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Spermacoce vulpina include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Spermacoce vulpina is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make a syrup or salve for topical application.
